scenes.text:
You stop where the darkness thickens.
The singing has stopped. Something old is listening.

Ahead, three shapes resolve out of the dark.
The lost ones. The Keeper has taken more than one.
Who do you reach for first?

choices.text: {missing_name} — the one the kingdom was looking for.
Moth — your companion, who shouldn't be here at all.
The Hedge Witch — chained in willow root, smiling at you.
{other_name} — the King's son, who rode out and never came back.
Ser Vale — knight of the castle, on his knees in the dark.
Rook — the road-mystery, bound at the wrists, not afraid.

scenes.text:
The torch flares. The dark eats half the light and spits the rest back.
The Keeper sees you now. The lost ones see you too — three faces turned in the gloom, blinking.

There is no time to be careful. You have to choose.

choices.text: Run for {missing_name}. Take her hand and don't stop.
Run for {other_name}. Drag him into the light with you.
Stand. Fight the Keeper here, in the open.

scenes.text:
The Keeper turns. Old and slow and hungry. It has been here a very long time, and it does not like that you have come.
You know who you came for. The question is how you leave.

choices.text: Steel. Cut the Keeper down where it stands.
Steel. Cut with the shadow blade — the dark won't see itself.
Words. The Keeper is old. Old things can be bargained with.
Trickery. The tunnel map shows a way the Keeper never learned.
Trickery. The signet is older than the Keeper. Show it.
Yourself. Stay. Let the others go.
Before stepping through, take inventory of self.
Walk on. (What came after.)
Walk on. (What came after.)
Walk on. (What came after.)
Walk on. (What came after.)
